journal_entry.description: Client entertainment meal paid with corporate credit card
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS, client entertainment meals are recorded as Meals and Entertainment Expense (operating expense). The corporate credit card creates a liability (Credit Card Payable). The transaction text explicitly states this was for client entertainment, removing any ambiguity about the business purpose. No tax is recoverable on client entertainment expenses in Canada per CRA rules.

output.reason: Client entertainment meal = expense increase. The $125 meal for client entertainment is a business expense (entertainment/meals expense), which is increased by debiting.

debit_classifier.reason: Client entertainment meal = expense increase. The $125 meal for client entertainment is a business expense (entertainment/meals expense), which is increased by debiting.

output.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase (accounts payable/credit card payable increases when a purchase is made on credit)
